prompt: design a pressure vessel for compressed nitrogen storage

intent.design_goal: Design a safe, cost-effective, high-pressure vessel for storing compressed nitrogen at specified service pressures and temperatures.
reference_objects: ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC), Section III, Chapter N550

constraints.description: Maximum working pressure must exceed anticipated service pressure by a safety factor of at least 1.5 to 2.0.
Vessel must operate reliably over the temperature range from ambient (~20°C) to the maximum expected temperature (~80°C).
Material must resist nitrogen corrosion, embrittlement, and maintain mechanical strength at designated pressures.
Vessel dimensions and mass should fit within the available storage footprint and comply with handling/transport constraints.
Design must remain within the project’s budgetary allocation for materials, fabrication, and testing.

unknowns: expected purity or impurities in nitrogen
desired steel grade or alternative material
number of pressure relief devices or rupture disks
intent.raw_input: design a pressure vessel for compressed nitrogen storage

requirement_spec.design_goal: Design a safe, cost-effective, high-pressure vessel for storing compressed nitrogen at specified service pressures and temperatures.

requirements.description: Design object type resolved as pressure_vessel
requirements.originating_text: design a pressure vessel for compressed nitrogen storage
downstream_design_consequences: Initializes constraint graph even when numeric targets are absent

requirement_spec.completeness_rationale: All required engineering decisions resolved from prompt.

functional_analysis.primary_function: Design a safe, cost‑effective high‑pressure vessel for storing compressed nitrogen that meets specified service pressures and temperature range while complying with material, size, weight, and cost constraints.

functions.name: determine_design_parameters
functions.description: Establish nominal operating pressure, temperature limits, safety factor, and service condition requirements.

functions.name: select_material
functions.description: Identify material(s) that provide resistance to nitrogen embrittlement, maintain strength at design conditions, and support required safety factor.

functions.name: structural_analysis
functions.description: Perform finite element or analytic calculations to verify vessel wall thickness and geometry provide required safety margin at maximum design pressure.

functions.name: thermal_performance_analysis
functions.description: Evaluate temperature rise, thermal expansion, and material property changes over the operational temperature range to ensure integrity.

functions.name: geometry_optimization
functions.description: Optimize vessel shape, dimensions, and mass to fit footprint constraints while minimizing material usage and cost.

functions.name: code_compliance
functions.description: Verify that design meets applicable pressure vessel codes, safety regulations, and industry standards.

functions.name: material_testing
functions.description: Conduct proof and fatigue tests on selected material specimens to validate performance under high pressure and temperature.

functions.name: fabrication_planning
functions.description: Plan manufacturing processes, welding techniques, and quality control to achieve cost efficiency and dimensional accuracy.

functions.name: assembly_and_testing
functions.description: Assemble vessel components and perform pressure and leak tests to qualify the final unit.

functions.name: cost_and_documentation
functions.description: Track material, fabrication, testing, and contingency costs; compile design reports and compliance documentation.

flows.description: Design pressure/temperature data informs material selection.
Functional sizing constraints used for geometry optimization.
Base cost estimate derives from design parameters.
Chosen material properties used in structural calculations.
Material thermal conductivity informs temperature analysis.
Material cost flows to cost tracking.
Material specification supplied to testing lab.
Structural analysis results used for code compliance.
Material test data provides evidence for compliance.
Test results included in cost and documentation.
Testing cost recorded in cost tracking.

required_assemblies.name: Material Selection Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Provide material that satisfies nitrogen compatibility, mechanical strength, and cost constraints.

required_assemblies.name: Structural Integrity Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Ensure vessel walls provide the required safety factor under maximum operating pressure.

required_assemblies.name: Performance Analysis Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Guarantee vessel operates reliably over the full temperature range and achieves acceptable thermal expansion.

required_assemblies.name: Manufacturing Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Plan fabrication, welding, and quality control to meet cost and dimensional targets.

required_assemblies.name: Cost and Documentation Assembly
required_assemblies.purpose: Track all cost elements, qualify the vessel, and compile compliance documentation.

component_names: Temperature Sensor Array and Monitoring System
Weld Joint Configuration for Stress Relief
Welding Quality Control and Inspection Plan
Pressure Test Fixture and Measurement System
